LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES (WRONGFUL DEATH-ASBESTOS). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OVERRULED AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT MODIFIES ITS RULING ON THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FROM SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND (SINCE PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL INDICATED THAT THEY WILL NOT BE PURSUING THAT CAUSE OF ACTION & THEREFORE NOT AMENDING). DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL INDICATED THAT HE WOULD SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS (PUNITIVE DAMAGES) OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES (WRONGFUL DEATH-ASBESTOS). ARGUED AND THE COURT DENIED THE MOTION TO STRIKE. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL INDICATED THAT HE WOULD SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: BELDON YEE, CSR #2647

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES (WRONGFUL DEATH-ASBESTOS).
APPEARANCE: Mia Mattis, Esq. of Harowitz & Tigerman, LLP 788-1588 for pltfs & opposing parties; Troy D. McMahan, Esq. of Filice Brown Eassa & McLeod LLP 510/444-3131 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - OVERRULED AS TO 1ST, 2ND, 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. The Court modifies its ruling on the 3rd cause of action from sustained with leave to amend to without leave to amend (since plaintiffs counsel indicated that they will not be pursuing that cause of action & therefore not amending). Defendant's counsel indicated that he would submit a proposed order.

DEFENDANT CHEVRON U.S.A. INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS (PUNITIVE DAMAGES) OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES (WRONGFUL DEATH-ASBESTOS).
Argued and the Court denied the motion to strike. Defendant's counsel indicated that he would submit a proposed order. (302/PHA/KD) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR #2647

MOTION Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And To Consolidate Survival And Wrongful Death Actions
Off Calendar Nov-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And To Consolidate Survival And Wrongful Death Actions.
APPEARANCE: Mia Mattis, Esq. of Harowitz & Tigerman, LLP 788-1588 for pltfs & moving parties; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANT SUBSTITUTING SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST (Yvonne Woods for decedent Robert Sims in #440439) PER CCP 377.32. GRANT CONSOLIDATION #440439 & #451136 (for all purposes). Lead case number shall be 440439. Order signed in open court. (302/REQ/AP) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR #2647
